query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "does vla even know the basics? measuring commonsense and world knowled" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on theoretical models of human knowledge, ignorance, and "knowledge entropy" based on group dynamics and individual psychology, which is a distinct construct from measuring commonsense and world knowledge in Vision-Language Action (VLA) models. It fails to meet any inclusion criteria as it does not address the specific domain (VLA/LLMs), the mechanism of measuring AI knowledge, or the empirical population of interest, representing only a homonym overlap on the word "knowledge."
